rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Intervenors¿ (on behalf of Scott Co. of California) motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration based on statements that contradict his deposition testimony are sustained. Plaintiff¿s statements in his declaration regarding asbestos content of the Monokote fireproofing at issue are inadmissible. (See Peterson depo., Newly Served Vol. I, 117:17-118:6.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS PEARSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Rheem Manufacturing Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is off calendar. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Even if Mr. Fuhrman¿s declaration is admissible, when read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony it merely creates a triable issue whether the heat exchangers plaintiff worked with and around were manufactured by Rheem. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
